attractions.name: Agecroft Hall & Gardens (Agecroft Hall & Gardens)
attractions.category: Museum / Landmark / Garden (Agecroft Hall & Gardens, Tripadvisor)
attractions.description: Agecroft Hall & Gardens is a 16th-century English Tudor manor house that was dismantled, shipped across the Atlantic, and reassembled in Richmond in the 1920s. Today, it serves as a museum and 23-acre estate showcasing five centuries of history, featuring authentic period furnishings and lavish formal gardens overlooking the James River (Agecroft Hall & Gardens, AAA).
attractions.address: 4305 Sulgrave Road, Richmond, VA 23221-3256 (Agecroft Hall & Gardens, Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Tuesday - Sunday: 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Last house tour at 4:00 PM). Closed Mondays and major holidays (Agecroft Hall & Gardens). Note: Gift shop open Thursday - Sunday 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (Agecroft Hall & Gardens).
attractions.admission: General Admission (includes self-guided tour of House Museum, Gardens, Woodland Walk, and Sunroom Exhibit): Adults $12, AAA/Seniors $11, Students $8, Children (6 & under) Free, Active Military (with ID) Free, Museums for All (SNAP recipients) Free. Garden Admission (Gardens, Woodland Walk & Sunroom Exhibit only): Adults $6, AAA/Seniors $5, Students $4, Children (6 & under) Free (Agecroft Hall & Gardens)
highlights: '16th-century Tudor manor house originally built in Lancashire, England ([Agecroft Hall & Gardens)', 'Sunken Garden inspired by designs at Hampton Court Palace (Agecroft Hall & Gardens)', 'Authentic period furnishings, tapestries, and leaded glass windows (AAA, CharlottesvilleFamily)', 'Annual home of the Richmond Shakespeare Festival (Lonely Planet)', '23 acres of formal gardens, woodland trails, and a turf maze (Agecroft Hall & Gardens)', 'Historical Knot Garden and aromatic Fragrance Garden (CharlottesvilleFamily)']
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets online in advance as time slots are limited. Wear comfortable walking shoes, but be aware there are many steps throughout the gardens which can be difficult for strollers. Picnicking is allowed on the grounds with garden admission, though no food service is available on-site (Agecroft Hall & Gardens, CharlottesvilleFamily).
attractions.nearby: Virginia Museum of Fine Arts (VMFA), Virginia Museum of History & Culture, Carytown shopping district, RVA Beer Trail, and various local eateries within one mile (Agecroft Hall & Gardens)

attractions.name: Altria Theater
attractions.description: Altria Theater is one of Richmond's premier live entertainment venues, featuring a grand 3,600-seat theater and a stunning 1,000-capacity ballroom. Originally built in 1927 in the Moorish Revival style, it hosts over 100 performances annually, including Broadway productions, concerts, and comedy shows Official Website.
attractions.hours: Box Office Windows: Monday - Friday, 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM. On performance days, the Box Office opens 2 hours prior to the advertised showtime Official Website.
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Box Office); Event-dependent (Performances) Official Website
attractions.admission: Ticket prices vary by event, typically starting from approximately $79 for Broadway shows and up to $198+ for premium events like 'Hamilton' SeatGeek. Season subscriptions are available for Broadway in Richmond performances Official Website.
highlights: 'Host to major Broadway in Richmond productions and The Richmond Forum [Official Website', 'Renowned for its excellent acoustics and unique Moorish Revival architecture Official Website', 'Features a grand ballroom with a capacity of 1,000 guests for private events Official Website', 'Centrally located on the Virginia Commonwealth University (VCU) campus across from Monroe Park Wikipedia', 'Official vehicle partnership with Sheehy Lexus of Richmond providing premium guest activations Official Website']
attractions.tips: Altria Theater is a cashless venue; all major credit and debit cards are accepted. It is recommended to arrive early as the Box Office opens 2 hours before shows and security screenings are required for entry Official Website.

attractions.name: American Civil War Museum at Historic Tredegar
attractions.description: The American Civil War Museum at Historic Tredegar features two floors of comprehensive exhibits detailing the causes, course, and consequences of the Civil War. Located on the historic site of the Tredegar Iron Works, the museum preserves the remains of what was once the Confederacy's most vital ordnance foundry (American Civil War Museum).
attractions.hours: Monday-Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (American Civil War Museum)
attractions.admission: Adults: $19, Seniors (65+), Teachers, & College Students: $17, Veterans & Active Military: $13, Youth (6-18): $10, Children under 6: Free. Multi-site passes range from $15-$40 (American Civil War Museum).
highlights: "'A People’s Contest' flagship exhibit featuring hundreds of original artifacts ([ACWM)", 'Immersive short-film experience in the Robins Theater (ACWM)', 'Historic 1861 Gun Foundry and rolling mill ruins (National Park Service)', "'The Impending Crisis' exhibition on pre-war tensions (ACWM)", "'Beyond Valor' exhibit focused on United States Colored Troops (ACWM)", 'Life-size bronze statue of Abraham Lincoln and his son Tad (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Start with the introductory film to establish context for the exhibits. Be sure to validate your parking ticket at the Guest Services desk to avoid fees (Tripadvisor). The museum is adjacent to scenic river trails, so wear comfortable walking shoes (Reddit).
attractions.nearby: Brown's Island, Belle Isle, Canal Walk, T. Tyler Potterfield Memorial Bridge, White House of the Confederacy (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Belle Isle (Wikipedia))
attractions.description: Belle Isle is a 54-acre island in the middle of the James River in downtown Richmond, Virginia). As a popular site within the James River Park System, it features wooded paths, historical industrial ruins, and the site of a notorious Civil War prisoner-of-war camp. Visitors can enjoy outdoor activities such as mountain biking, rock climbing, and sunbathing on large granite rocks along the river (Visit Richmond VA).
attractions.address: 300 Tredegar St, Richmond, VA 23219 (Virginia Department of Wildlife Resources)
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise - Sunset, Tuesday: Sunrise - Sunset, Wednesday: Sunrise - Sunset, Thursday: Sunrise - Sunset, Friday: Sunrise - Sunset, Saturday: Sunrise - Sunset, Sunday: Sunrise - Sunset (Friends of James River Park)
attractions.admission: Free (Virginia Department of Wildlife Resources)
highlights: 'Suspension footbridge with panoramic river and city views ([Wikipedia))', 'Historic remains of a 19th-century iron foundry and hydroelectric plant (Friends of James River Park)', 'Site of a significant Civil War-era Union prisoner-of-war camp (Venture Richmond)', 'Mountain bike skills area and beginner-to-intermediate trails (Venture Richmond)', 'Large granite rocks ideal for sunbathing and watching the Hollywood Rapids (Visit Richmond VA)', 'Wheelchair-accessible fishing at the quarry pond (Friends of James River Park)']
attractions.tips: Access the island via the suspension footbridge under the Robert E. Lee Bridge on Tredegar Street. While the rocks are popular for sunbathing, the Hollywood Rapids are dangerous, and swimming is strictly discouraged in that area (Visit Richmond VA). Visitors should bring their own water, though a filling station is available near the bridge (Friends of James River Park).
attractions.nearby: Hollywood Cemetery, American Civil War Museum at Historic Tredegar, Brown's Island, Canal Walk

attractions.name: Berkeley Plantation
attractions.description: Berkeley Plantation is one of Virginia's most historic estates, featuring a 1726 Georgian mansion built for the Harrison family. It is celebrated as the site of the first official Thanksgiving in America and is the birthplace of both a signer of the Declaration of Independence and the ninth U.S. President. Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.address: 12602 Harrison Landing Road, Charles City, Virginia 23030
attractions.hours: March 14 – Nov 13: 9:30 AM – 4:30 PM (Grounds until 5:00 PM); Nov 14 – Jan 1: 9:30 AM – 3:30 PM (Grounds until 4:00 PM); Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, and Easter Sunday Official Website
attractions.days_open: Open daily from mid-March through New Year's Day (Closed January 2 to mid-March) Official Website
attractions.admission: Adults: $19.00, Seniors (60+): $17.50, Children (6-16): $8.00, Children under 6: Free, Military: $17.50 (Adult) / $7.50 (Child), Annual Pass: $50.00 (Adult) / $25.00 (Child) Official Website
highlights: 'Birthplace of Benjamin Harrison V (Declaration signer) and President William Henry Harrison [Official Website', 'Site of the first official Thanksgiving in America in 1619 Official Website', "Where the bugle call 'Taps' was composed in 1862 Official Website", 'Five-terraced formal gardens extending to the banks of the James River Official Website', 'Basement museum housing Native American, Colonial, and Civil War artifacts Official Website']
attractions.tips: Allow at least 2 hours for your visit; photography is prohibited inside the mansion and museum; bring a picnic to enjoy on the grounds; start with the orientation film in the Coach House. Official Website, Coast2CoastWithKids

attractions.name: Black History Museum and Cultural Center of Virginia (BHMVA) (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The mission of the Black History Museum & Cultural Center of Virginia is to share, interpret, preserve, exhibit, and commemorate the rich history and culture of African Americans throughout the Commonwealth of Virginia and beyond. Housed in the historic Leigh Street Armory, the museum tells stories that are often untold and forgotten, actively contributing to the ongoing narrative through scholarship and curation. (Official Website)
attractions.address: 122 West Leigh Street, Richmond, VA 23220 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Sunday - Tuesday & Holidays: CLOSED (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults: $10, Seniors/Students w/ID: $8, Children 4-12: $6, Children under 4: Free, Military Discount: 10% (Official Website)
highlights: 'Housed in the historic Leigh Street Armory, built in 1895 for an African American militia unit ([National Park Service)', "Permanent exhibitions featuring interactive components, artifacts, and documents highlighting Virginia's Black history (Tripadvisor)", 'Extensive Black history timeline and exhibits on notable figures like Arthur Ashe, Wendell Scott, and the Tuskegee Airmen (Official Website)', 'A designated member of the African American Civil Rights Network since 2024 (Official Website)', 'Rotating featured exhibitions such as "A Prescription for Change: Black Voices Shaping Healthcare in Virginia" (Official Website)', 'Regular community programs including the Kidz@122 youth education series (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Visitors are encouraged to purchase tickets in advance online to secure their preferred time. Guided tours can be requested through the website for a more detailed educational experience. The museum often hosts community events at the nearby Abner Clay Park, so check their calendar before visiting. (Official Website)

attractions.name: Bon Air Memorial Rose Garden
attractions.description: Bon Air Memorial Rose Garden is a picturesque 24-acre park in Arlington, Virginia, that serves as a living memorial to local veterans. It features over 120 varieties of roses and approximately 2,500 plants, along with charming gazebos and manicured walking paths. The park also includes diverse botanical displays like azalea, shade, and ornamental tree gardens, making it a premier destination for photography and peaceful nature walks (Arlington County, Arlington Magazine).
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Sunrise to Sunset (Arlington County)
attractions.admission: Free (Arlington County)
highlights: 'Over 120 distinct rose varieties and 2,500 individual plants ([Arlington County)', 'Memorial garden dedicated to World War II veterans (Arlington County)', 'Iconic architectural features including gazebos, pergolas, and stone paths (Explore DC)', 'Diverse botanical collections such as azalea, sun, shade, and ornamental tree gardens (Arlington County)', 'Direct access to the Washington & Old Dominion (W&OD) and Four Mile Run trails (Tripadvisor)', 'Popular site for professional and personal photography sessions (Katy Murray Photography)']
attractions.tips: Peak bloom for the roses typically occurs from late May through June, though flowers remain visible until mid-October (Arlington County). While personal photography is free, professional or commercial shoots require a permit from Arlington County (Explore DC). Visitors are encouraged to arrive early on weekends as the on-site parking lot can fill up quickly, and smoking is prohibited throughout the park (Arlington County).
attractions.nearby: Bluemont Park, Upton Hill Regional Park, Four Mile Run Trail, W&OD Railroad Regional Park, and the Ballston neighborhood (Arlington County, Tripadvisor)

attractions.description: Capital One Arena is a premier indoor sports and entertainment venue located in the heart of Washington, D.C.'s vibrant Penn Quarter neighborhood. Opened in 1997, the arena serves as the home for the NHL's Washington Capitals and the NBA's Washington Wizards, hosting more than 200 events annually including concerts, family shows, and collegiate sports. attractions.name: Dogwood Dell
attractions.description: Dogwood Dell is a 2,400-seat outdoor amphitheatre that serves as a cornerstone of Richmond's cultural landscape. Since 1956, it has hosted the summer-long Festival of Arts, featuring a diverse array of free concerts, theatrical productions, and dance performances under the stars. (Richmond Gov)

attractions.name: Fredericksburg Historic District (Old Town Fredericksburg)
attractions.description: The Fredericksburg Historic District is a 40-block area encompassing the city's original 50-acre town site from 1728, featuring over 350 buildings from the 18th and 19th centuries. It serves as the community's social, political, and cultural core, showcasing outstanding examples of Colonial, Federal, and Victorian architecture along the Rappahannock River. Fredericksburg, VA Official Website, DHR Virginia

Visitors can explore 14 re-created structures and a Powhatan Indian village, engaging with costumed interpreters who demonstrate 17th-century life, trade, and culture. Wikipedia, Chesterfield County

attractions.description: Established in 1847, Hollywood Cemetery is a historic rural cemetery and registered Level 1 Arboretum overlooking the James River in Richmond, Virginia (Hollywood Cemetery). Designed by architect John Notman, it features a park-like "garden" layout with winding paths, stately trees, and impressive Victorian monuments (Wikipedia)). It serves as both an active burial ground and a popular outdoor museum, famously housing the remains of two U.S. presidents (Hollywood Cemetery).
attractions.address: 412 South Cherry Street, Richmond, VA 23220 Hollywood Cemetery
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM Daily; 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM Daily during Daylight Saving Time Hollywood Cemetery
attractions.admission: Free Hollywood Cemetery
highlights: 'James Monroe’s Tomb ("The Birdcage"), a National Historic Landmark [Hollywood Cemetery', 'The Confederate Pyramid, a 90-foot mortarless granite monument honoring 18,000 soldiers Virginia.org', 'President John Tyler’s Monument and the grave of Jefferson Davis Hollywood Cemetery', 'The Iron Dog, a famous cast-iron statue with many local legends Hollywood Cemetery', 'Scenic overlooks providing panoramic views of the James River and Richmond skyline Hollywood Cemetery', 'Registered Level 1 Arboretum featuring heritage roses and historic trees Hollywood Cemetery']
attractions.tips: Visitors can purchase a paper map for $1 at the cemetery office to help navigate the 135-acre grounds (Hollywood Cemetery). Please be respectful of ongoing funeral services and grieving families in this active cemetery (Hollywood Cemetery). Dogs are welcome but must remain on a leash, and skateboards or one-wheels are strictly prohibited (Hollywood Cemetery). Wear comfortable shoes as the terrain is hilly and expansive (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Jackson Ward Historic District
attractions.category: Historic District / Neighborhood / National Historic Landmark District National Park Service
attractions.description: Jackson Ward is a nationally significant historic district in Richmond, Virginia, famously known as the "Black Wall Street of America" and the "Harlem of the South." During the early 20th century, it was a thriving center for African American commerce, entertainment, and religion, and it remains one of the largest historic districts in the U.S. associated with Black culture. Today, it is a vibrant urban neighborhood featuring beautifully preserved architecture, soul food institutions, and cultural landmarks. Wikipedia, City of Richmond
attractions.address: Bounded by I-95/CSX tracks (north), Broad Street (south), 4th Street (east), and Belvidere Street (west) Historic Jackson Ward Association
attractions.hours: District/Neighborhood: Open 24/7; Maggie L. Walker NHS Visitor Center: Tuesday–Saturday 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM; Black History Museum: Tuesday–Saturday 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM National Park Service, Black History Museum
attractions.admission: Free (Public Historic District); specific sites like the Maggie L. Walker National Historic Site are also free National Park Service, while others like the Black History Museum may have fees Black History Museum
highlights: 'Maggie L. Walker National Historic Site, preserving the home of the first African American woman to charter a bank [National Park Service', 'The Black History Museum and Cultural Center of Virginia, located in the historic Leigh Street Armory Black History Museum', 'The iconic Bill "Bojangles" Robinson Statue at the intersection of Adams and Leigh Streets Wikipedia', 'The Hippodrome Theater, a historic jazz venue that hosted legends like Ella Fitzgerald and Duke Ellington Discover Richmond Tours', "Annual 2nd Street Festival, a massive celebration of the neighborhood's heritage held every October Tripadvisor", 'Distinctive architecture featuring elaborate cast-iron porches similar to those in New Orleans DHR Virginia']
attractions.tips: Download the NPS App for a self-guided podcast tour of the historic district's landmarks. Plan to dine at local favorites like Mama J's for authentic soul food, and be aware that street parking is often limited to two hours without a permit. National Park Service, City of Richmond

attractions.name: James River Park System
attractions.description: The James River Park System is a sprawling 600-acre urban oasis that follows the banks of the James River as it flows through the heart of Richmond. It offers a unique combination of pristine wilderness, historical ruins, and adventure recreation, including the only Class IV whitewater rapids found in an urban downtown setting in the United States (City of Richmond, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise to Sunset; Tuesday: Sunrise to Sunset; Wednesday: Sunrise to Sunset; Thursday: Sunrise to Sunset; Friday: Sunrise to Sunset; Saturday: Sunrise to Sunset; Sunday: Sunrise to Sunset (City of Richmond)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Richmond, Virginia DWR)
highlights: 'Class IV whitewater rapids within sight of downtown skyscrapers ([Wikipedia)', 'Belle Isle, featuring a pedestrian suspension bridge and historical iron works ruins (Virginia DWR)', 'Over 22 miles of multi-use trails for hiking, running, and mountain biking (Friends of James River Park)', 'Pony Pasture Rapids, a popular spot for swimming, kayaking, and sunbathing on river rocks (Wikipedia)', 'Outdoor rock climbing at the Manchester Climbing Wall and Buttermilk trail areas (City of Richmond)', 'Historical structures like the Pumphouse and remnants of the James River & Kanawha Canal (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: Visitors are required to wear a life jacket (PFD) when river levels are at 5 feet or higher, and only expert paddlers should be on the river when levels reach 9 feet. Glass containers and alcohol are strictly prohibited throughout the park system, and dogs must remain on a leash in most sections (City of Richmond).

attractions.name: Jamestown Settlement
attractions.description: Jamestown Settlement is a world-class living-history museum that tells the story of 17th-century Virginia and the convergence of Powhatan Indian, English, and West Central African cultures. Visitors can explore immersive indoor galleries featuring rare artifacts and films before stepping outside into expansive re-creations of historical sites. The museum provides a hands-on look at the daily lives of early settlers and indigenous people through interactive demonstrations and historical interpreters (Jamestown-Yorktown Foundation).

attractions.name: Petersburg National Battlefield
attractions.description: Petersburg National Battlefield preserves the sites of the 292-day Siege of Petersburg, a pivotal Civil War campaign that resulted in over 70,000 casualties and the eventual fall of the Confederate capital, Richmond. The 2,700-acre park encompasses multiple units, including the Eastern Front, Five Forks Battlefield, and General Grant's Headquarters at City Point. Visitors can explore well-preserved earthworks, historic structures, and extensive trails that tell the story of the longest siege in American military history. (National Park Service, American Battlefield Trust)

attractions.description: The Virginia War Memorial is the Commonwealth's premier monument, museum, and educational center dedicated to honoring Virginians who served in the U.S. Armed Forces from World War II through today. It serves as a place of remembrance and reflection, featuring a shrine with nearly 12,000 names of fallen heroes etched in glass and stone (Virginia War Memorial).
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 9:00 a.m. - 4:00 p.m.; Sunday: 12:00 p.m. - 4:00 p.m. (Shrine is open daily 5:00 a.m. - Midnight) (Virginia War Memorial, DVS)
attractions.admission: Free (Virginia War Memorial)

Bruce Heilman Amphitheater overlooking the James River and downtown skyline (Virginia War Memorial)', "'Virginians at War' documentary series shown in the Reynolds Theater (Virginia War Memorial)", 'USS Birmingham Library and archives of oral histories (Virginia War Memorial)']
attractions.tips: Visit the Shrine of Memory at dusk for a stunning view of the skyline. Ask the volunteer docents, many of whom are veterans, for personal stories and insights. Parking is free on-site and easily accessible from Belvidere Street or Rowe Street (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Virginia War Memorial Carillon
attractions.description: The Virginia War Memorial Carillon is a 240-foot tall Georgian Revival bell tower dedicated in 1932 to honor the Virginians who died in World War I. Located in William Byrd Park, it houses a massive musical instrument of 53 bronze bells and serves as a historic monument and museum space. (Carillon Civic Association, Virginia War Memorial)
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM (Virginia War Memorial)
attractions.admission: Free (Virginia War Memorial)

attractions.tips: Visit during patriotic holidays (July 4th, Veterans Day) for live bell concerts. Climb the front steps to the terrace for panoramic views of Byrd Park and the surrounding neighborhood. (Carillon Civic Association)

attractions.name: Westover Plantation (Historic Westover)
attractions.description: Westover is a National Historic Landmark and colonial tidewater plantation established circa 1730 by William Byrd II, the founder of Richmond. The estate is renowned for its elegant Georgian architecture, including the famous "Westover Doorway," and its expansive grounds featuring formal gardens and historic outbuildings overlooking the James River. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Grounds and Gardens: 10:00 AM – 4:30 PM daily. Interior Tours: By appointment only. (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Grounds (Self-guided): Adults $5.00, Youth (7–16) $2.00, Children (6 and under) Free, Active and Veteran Military $3.00, Family $15.00. Interior Tours: $30.00/person (by appointment). (Official Website)
highlights: 'The Westover Doorway, a Portland stone entrance considered one of the most copied in America ([Official Website)', 'Formal gardens featuring the tomb of William Byrd II marked by a marble obelisk (Official Website)', 'Exquisite 18th-century iron gates and a rare iron "clairvoyee" fence with symbolic icons (Official Website)', 'Historic outbuildings including a five-hole privy and an original smokehouse (Official Website)', 'Underground escape tunnels originally built to protect against attacks (Official Website)', 'Scenic riverfront views along the north bank of the James River (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: The main house is a private residence and only open for interior tours by appointment or during special events like Historic Garden Week. Visitors can pay admission via an honor box (cash), check, or Venmo (@Westover-Gardens). Picnics are permitted on the grounds, but visitors must pack out all trash. (Official Website)

attractions.name: Yorktown Battlefield (Part of Colonial National Historical Park) National Park Service
attractions.category: Landmark, Park, and Museum National Park Service
attractions.description: Yorktown Battlefield preserves the site of the final major land battle of the American Revolutionary War, where General George Washington and his French allies secured the British surrender in 1781. Visitors can explore the preserved siege lines, earthworks, and historic structures that define this pivotal moment in American history. The park offers museum exhibits, self-guided driving tours, and ranger-led programs that interpret the victory that ensured United States independence National Park Service
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Saturday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Sunday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM. Battlefield tour roads are open daily until sunset National Park Service
attractions.days_open: Daily, year-round with the exception of Thanksgiving Day, December 25, and January 1 National Park Service
attractions.admission: $15.00 for Adults (16 and older), Free for Children (15 and under). Entrance passes are valid for 7 days and include entry to both Yorktown Battlefield and Historic Jamestowne. The Colonial National Historical Park Annual Pass is available for $45.00 National Park Service
highlights: 'Yorktown Battlefield Visitor Center and Museum Exhibits [NPS', 'Self-guided driving tours of the British and Allied siege lines NPS', 'The Moore House, where the surrender negotiations took place NPS', "Surrender Field, site of the British army's formal capitulation NPS", "The Nelson House, a Georgian mansion that served as Cornwallis's headquarters NPS", 'Yorktown National Cemetery, established in 1866 NPS']
attractions.tips: Download the free Yorktown Battlefield Tour Guide App for audio commentary during the driving tour. Check the seasonal artillery firing schedule for live demonstrations and arrive early for ranger-led tours, which depart daily from the visitor center National Park Service
attractions.nearby: Historic Jamestowne, Colonial Williamsburg, Yorktown Beach, Riverfront Landing, and the American Revolution Museum at Yorktown National Park Service

attractions.name: Church Hill (St. John's Church Historic District) Wikipedia

It is famously known as the site of St. John's Episcopal Church, where Patrick Henry gave his 'Give me liberty or give me death!' speech during the Second Virginia Convention in 1775. Today, the area is celebrated for its well-preserved 19th-century architecture, scenic parks, and a vibrant, award-winning culinary scene Church Hill Association, Wikipedia.

attractions.tips: Visit on summer Sundays to witness professional actors perform live 'Liberty or Death' reenactments inside the church. Wear comfortable walking shoes to navigate the neighborhood's steep hills and historic cobblestone streets. Arrive early to Libby Hill Park to catch a sunset view of the James River and city skyline Historic St. John's Church, Discover Richmond Tours.

attractions.name: Richmond Slave Trail
attractions.description: The Richmond Slave Trail is a 2.5-mile self-guided walking route that chronicles the history of the trade of enslaved Africans in Richmond from 1775 until 1865. The trail begins at the Manchester Docks, a major port in the domestic slave trade, and traces a path through the former slave markets of Shockoe Bottom to the First African Baptist Church. Along the way, interpretive markers detail the somber history of human trafficking, forced labor, and the resilience of the enslaved community. Virginia.org, Dogwood
attractions.hours: Monday: Sunrise to Sunset; Tuesday: Sunrise to Sunset; Wednesday: Sunrise to Sunset; Thursday: Sunrise to Sunset; Friday: Sunrise to Sunset; Saturday: Sunrise to Sunset; Sunday: Sunrise to Sunset James River Park
attractions.admission: Free Visit Richmond, Virginia.org
highlights: ["Manchester Docks (Ancarrow's Landing)
The starting point where enslaved Africans were brought ashore before being marched into the city.", 'Reconciliation Statue
A 15-foot bronze monument commemorating the international triangular slave trade.', "Lumpkin's Slave Jail (Devil's Half Acre)
The site of an infamous holding facility and auction house for enslaved people.", 'African Burial Ground
A sacred site that served as the first designated cemetery for people of African descent in Richmond.', 'Henry "Box" Brown Marker
Commemorates the man who escaped slavery by shipping himself to freedom in a wooden crate.', "First African Baptist Church
The trail's terminus and a historic center for African American spiritual and social life."]
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes for the 2.5-mile trek and bring plenty of water, as the trail is entirely outdoors. It is highly recommended to download a trail map beforehand, as markers can be difficult to locate in the busier urban sections of Shockoe Bottom. Starting at Manchester Docks allows visitors to follow the historical narrative of the enslaved experience in chronological order. Tripadvisor

As a National Historic Landmark, it serves as a museum showcasing the life and legacy of the 'Great Chief Justice' and holds the largest collection of Marshall family possessions. (Preservation Virginia, Wikipedia)

The museum complex includes the Old Stone House, Richmond’s oldest standing residential building, and an Enchanted Garden inspired by Poe's poetry (Poe Museum, Poe Museum).

attractions.name: Virginia Holocaust Museum (Official Website)
attractions.description: Founded in 1997, the Virginia Holocaust Museum is a poignant institution dedicated to depicting the Holocaust through personal stories and documenting the atrocities of World War II (Official Website). Housed in a historic 120,000-square-foot tobacco warehouse, the museum employs its unique exhibits and programming to teach about the dangers of hate, prejudice, and indifference (Richmond City). It serves as a moving memorial to the millions of victims who perished while inspiring future generations to fight intolerance (Travelingmitch).
attractions.address: 2000 East Cary Street, Richmond, Virginia 23223 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday–Friday: 9 AM – 5 PM; Saturday–Sunday: 11 AM – 5 PM (Official Website)
attractions.days_open: Open seven days a week (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Adults 18+: $10; Members, Children, Students, and Active Military: Free (Official Website)
highlights: 'Dimensions in Testimony: An interactive biography collection enabling visitors to have real-time conversations with pre-recorded video interviews of Holocaust survivors ([Official Website).', "Authentic Box Car: A German 'Güterwagen' box car used during the Holocaust for deportations, located right outside the museum's front doors (Official Website).", 'Children’s Memorial: A somber exhibit honoring the 1.5 million Jewish children who perished, featuring a panorama of empty classroom desks (Official Website).', 'Jewish-American Hall of Fame: The permanent home for this exhibit featuring portraits of over 50 Jewish Americans who made significant contributions to humanity (Official Website).', "Ipson Saga: Detailed exhibits telling the story of the Ipson family's survival in a crawl space during the Holocaust (Wikipedia).", 'Nuremberg Courtroom: A reconstruction and exhibit dedicated to the trials of Nazi war criminals (Wikipedia).']
attractions.tips: Allow at least 2 hours to thoroughly explore the exhibits (Tripadvisor). Free off-street parking is provided directly across from the museum on Cary Street (Official Website). The museum content is generally recommended for ages 12 and up due to its mature themes (The History Mom).

attractions.description: Monument Avenue is a grand, tree-lined residential boulevard and National Historic Landmark renowned for its diverse architectural styles and park-like median. Originally established in the late 19th century as a prestigious residential area, it features a collection of historic mansions, townhouses, and churches. Following the removal of its Confederate statues in 2021, it continues to serve as a significant cultural landscape, now anchored by the Arthur Ashe Monument. (Wikipedia, Virginia Department of Historic Resources)

attractions.description: The Answer Brewpub is Richmond's premier destination for experimental craft beer, authentic Vietnamese fusion, and retro arcade gaming. Founded in 2014 by James Beard semi-finalist An Bui, the 12,000-square-foot brewery features 70 rotating taps, including its award-winning "Joose" sours. The vibrant establishment offers a community-driven atmosphere with live music, a nostalgic game room, and a menu highlighted by fresh-baked Banh Mi (Official Website).
attractions.address: 6008 West Broad Street, Richmond, VA 23230 (Official Website)
attractions.hours: Monday-Wednesday: 4pm-12am, Thursday-Saturday: 12pm-12am, Sunday: 12pm-10pm (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Website)
highlights: '70 rotating taps featuring house-brewed "Joose" sours, "Andall" stouts, and guest beers ([Official Website)', 'Authentic Vietnamese fusion menu including fresh-baked Banh Mi, Rocket Shrimp, and "Wake N Bacon" jasmine fried rice (Official Website)', 'Large 12,000 sq. ft. brewery restaurant with two bars and a stage for live music (Official Website, Visit Richmond VA)', 'Nostalgic retro arcade featuring pinball, air hockey, and classic cabinet games (Official Website)', 'First Asian-owned craft brewery in Richmond, expanding on the legacy of the neighboring Mekong restaurant (Official Website)']
attractions.tips: The main entrance is located at the rear of the building, off the main parking lot. It is highly recommended to park in the back lot to avoid the long walk around from Broad Street. The venue is family-friendly and popular for watching sports or gathering with large groups (Official Website, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Mekong Vietnamese Restaurant (Adjacent), Willow Lawn Shopping Center, Lewis Ginter Botanical Garden, Maymont Park (Official Website, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: William Byrd Park (City of Richmond, Wikipedia)
attractions.description: William Byrd Park is a premier 287-acre urban park in Richmond's Near West End, offering a blend of natural beauty and historic landmarks. It features three man-made lakes, extensive walking and exercise trails, and serves as a major hub for community recreation and seasonal arts festivals (City of Richmond, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 600 South Arthur Ashe Boulevard, Richmond, VA 23220 (City of Richmond, DWR Virginia)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Sunrise to sunset (tennis complex available for anytime play) (City of Richmond)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Richmond, Virginia.org)
highlights: 'Three scenic man-made lakes: Fountain Lake, Swan Lake, and Shields Lake ([City of Richmond)', 'The World War I Memorial Carillon, a 240-foot tall historic bell tower (Wikipedia, The Rerva)', 'Dogwood Dell Amphitheater, hosting the annual Festival of the Arts and free summer concerts (City of Richmond)', 'The one-mile VITA exercise trail equipped with various fitness stations (Airial Travel)', 'Seasonal activities including pedal boat rentals on Fountain Lake and urban fishing (City of Richmond)', 'The historic Byrd Park Pump House, a notable Gothic Revival architectural gem (Airial Travel)']
attractions.tips: Visit early in the morning for peaceful sunrise views over the lakes. Be prepared to navigate around goose droppings on paved paths, especially near Fountain Lake, and consider bringing a picnic to enjoy at one of the many lakeside benches or shelters (Evendo, Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: Maymont Park, Carytown, Virginia Museum of Fine Arts (VMFA), James River Park System (Airial Travel, Wikipedia)

attractions.description: Yellow Umbrella Provisions is a cherished family-owned specialty grocery store and gourmet market that has been serving the Richmond community since 1986. They specialize in providing sustainably-sourced seafood, pasture-raised meats, house-made prepared foods, and a curated selection of wine and beer. Yellow Umbrella RVA

attractions.admission: Free (Specialty grocery store and market) Yellow Umbrella RVA
highlights: 'Sustainably-sourced fresh seafood and pasture-raised meats [Yellow Umbrella RVA', 'Famous scratch-made Lobster Rolls served at the Lunch Counter Yellow Umbrella RVA', 'Wide variety of house-made prepared foods and gourmet sides Yellow Umbrella RVA', 'Hand-rolled sushi prepared fresh by an onsite sushi chef Yellow Umbrella RVA', 'Curated selection of local provisions, craft beer, and fine wines Yellow Umbrella RVA', 'Full-service event catering and private in-store events Yellow Umbrella RVA']
attractions.tips: Check out the lunch counter for their daily specials, but arrive early as popular items like the lobster rolls can sell out. Don't forget to browse the 'Fishcow' section for a unique blend of butcher and seafood offerings. Yellow Umbrella RVA

It features a full-service 180-seat restaurant and a massive 200-seat outdoor deck offering some of the best views of the Richmond skyline and James River. Legend Brewing Company, RICtoday

attractions.name: Manchester Neighborhood (Old Manchester)
attractions.category: Landmark / Historic Neighborhood / Residential & Commercial District
attractions.description: Manchester is a historic and rapidly revitalizing neighborhood located on the south bank of the James River in Richmond, Virginia. Formerly an independent city until its merger with Richmond in 1910, it is now characterized by its blend of 19th-century industrial architecture, modern loft apartments, and a vibrant arts and brewery scene. The area is a hub for outdoor enthusiasts, offering direct access to the James River Park System, scenic floodwall walks, and unique urban climbing opportunities. Venture Richmond, Wikipedia
attractions.address: Hull Street Corridor (Central point near 920 Hull St), Richmond, VA 23224
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Individual business and attraction hours vary; typically 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM for offices) Venture Richmond
attractions.admission: Free Venture Richmond
highlights: ['Manchester Climbing Wall
A unique outdoor rock climbing destination set against the city skyline.', 'Floodwall Walk
Offers stunning panoramic views of the James River and downtown Richmond.', 'Art Works
A contemporary art center featuring four galleries and over 80 artist studios.', 'Manchester Slave Trail
A historic walking trail chronicling the history of the enslaved people who were brought to Richmond.', 'Legend Brewing Company
The oldest craft brewery in the Richmond region, known for its large deck with river views.', 'Historic Manchester Courthouse
A landmark building that remains a center for local judicial services.']
attractions.tips: Visit on the first Friday of the month for gallery openings at Art Works; use the parking area just south of the Mayo Bridge for the easiest access to the Floodwall Walk; check out the monthly 'Manchester Manifest' community celebrations. Venture Richmond, Trulia
attractions.nearby: Belle Isle, Brown’s Island, Ancarrow’s Landing, James River Park System, Shockoe Slip Venture Richmond

attractions.name: Wilton House Museum
attractions.description: Wilton House Museum is an 18th-century Georgian-style plantation manor built c. 1753 for the prominent Randolph family. Saved from industrial development in 1933, the house was dismantled and relocated to its current bluff overlooking the James River, where it now serves as a beautifully preserved site for colonial architecture and decorative arts (Wilton House Museum, Virginia Museum of History & Culture).
attractions.hours: Wednesday - Saturday: 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM; Sunday: 1:00 PM – 4:00 PM (Tours begin on the hour; last tour at 3:00 PM) (Wilton House Museum, Tripadvisor)
attractions.admission: General: Free; Student/Senior/AAA: Free; Children six and younger: Free; Active duty military: Free; Museums For All (SNAP/EBT): Free; Teachers: Free; WAPCA neighborhood members: Free; NSCDA Members: Free; Group Tours: $10.00 per adult, $5.00 per child (Wilton House Museum)
highlights: 'Authentic 18th-century Georgian-style architecture with original fully paneled interiors ([DHR)', 'Collection of 1,400 artifacts including furnishings, textiles, glass, and ceramics (Wikipedia)', 'Unique history of being completely dismantled and relocated 14-15 miles in 1933 (Wilton House Museum)', 'Stunning terraced grounds and gardens overlooking the James River (Wilton House Museum)', 'Educational exhibits on the Randolph family and the lives of the enslaved workers who lived there (Great American Treasures)']
attractions.tips: Tours begin on the hour and last 45 minutes; the last tour starts at 3:00 PM. The museum is accessible by stairs only. Leashed pets are welcome on the grounds but not inside the house (Wilton House Museum).
attractions.nearby: Agecroft Hall & Gardens, Virginia House, Mary Munford Playground, Virginia Museum of Fine Arts (VMFA), Virginia Museum of History & Culture (The History Mom)

attractions.name: Windsor Farms
attractions.description: Windsor Farms is an early 20th-century planned residential neighborhood in Richmond, Virginia, meticulously modeled after a traditional English village. Established in 1926, the area is renowned for its winding streets, lush common areas, and historic relocated English manor houses like Agecroft Hall and Virginia House. It offers a peaceful, pedestrian-friendly environment with significant architectural character and scenic views of the James River (Windsor Farms, Homes.com).
attractions.hours: Neighborhood: Open 24/7; Association Office: Mon-Fri 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Agecroft Hall: Thu-Sun 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (RVA.gov, Agecroft Hall)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Sunday (Neighborhood); Monday - Friday (Office) (Windsor Farms, RVA.gov)
attractions.admission: Free (Neighborhood); Agecroft Hall: $12 Adults, $11 Seniors (65+), $8 Students; Virginia House: Admission varies by event/tour (Agecroft Hall, Visit Richmond)
highlights: ['Modeled after a 16th-century English village with winding roads and Tudor-style architecture', 'Home to Agecroft Hall, a 15th-century English manor relocated piece-by-piece from Lancashire', 'Features Virginia House, a historic English country house overlooking the James River', 'Includes 11 beautifully maintained common areas totaling 20 acres of green space', 'The historic 1926 Lych Gate serves as a distinctive landmark at the neighborhood entrance', "Strict architectural standards preserve the neighborhood's original character and heritage"]
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable shoes for walking the scenic circular street patterns and hilly terrain. Visit during the spring to experience the vibrant cherry blossoms and magnolias in bloom along Windsor Way. Always respect the privacy of local residents while exploring the public sidewalks and common areas (Reddit, Homes.com).
attractions.nearby: Carytown, Maymont, Virginia Museum of Fine Arts (VMFA), Museum District (Chris Small Group)

attractions.name: Colonial Williamsburg (Colonial Williamsburg)
attractions.category: Living History Museum, Historic Landmark (Colonial Williamsburg)
attractions.description: Colonial Williamsburg is the world's largest living history museum, representing the restored 18th-century capital of Britain’s Virginia colony. Across 301 acres, visitors can engage with historic interpreters, watch master artisans at work, and explore meticulously preserved buildings that tell the story of early America (Colonial Williamsburg).
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Historic Trades & Sites, Art Museums). Evening programs run into the night (Colonial Williamsburg)
attractions.days_open: Open daily, 365 days a year (Colonial Williamsburg)
attractions.admission: Single-day Ticket (Online): Adult (13+) $33.75, Youth (6-12) $9.00; Multiday Ticket: Adult $55.00, Youth $28.00; Annual Pass: Adult $75.00, Youth $35.00; Historic Triangle Ticket: Adult $126.00, Youth $57.00; Children (0-5): Free (Colonial Williamsburg)
highlights: 'Historic Trades: Master artisans demonstrate 18th-century crafts using period tools ([Colonial Williamsburg)', "Governor's Palace: The grand residence of Virginia's royal governors (Colonial Williamsburg)", 'Art Museums: Home to the Abby Aldrich Rockefeller Folk Art Museum and DeWitt Wallace Decorative Arts Museum (Colonial Williamsburg)', 'The Capitol: The site of revolutionary political debates and early American lawmaking (Colonial Williamsburg)', 'Evening Programs: Ghost tours like Haunted Williamsburg and seasonal storytelling (Colonial Williamsburg)', 'Rare Breeds: 18th-century livestock varieties maintained on the grounds (Colonial Williamsburg)']
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes and layers for variable weather. Park for free at the Visitor Center and use the complimentary shuttle. Look for the Grand Union Flag to identify open buildings. Buy tickets online to save 10% on single-day admission before May 31 (Colonial Williamsburg).
attractions.nearby: Jamestown, Yorktown, Busch Gardens Williamsburg, Water Country USA, College of William & Mary (Colonial Williamsburg)
